highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 brings allocation-free hot paths, span-friendly APIs, and m151 stability fixes.
highlights_body: Two performance improvements eliminate per-call allocations in `SKFourByteTag.Parse` and `SKColor` hex parsing. The bundled engine folds in two rounds of Skia m151 upstream bug fixes, and `GRContextOptions` drops its unnecessary unsafe modifier.

bullets.lead: Skia m151 upstream bug fixes (two syncs)
bullets.detail: Two rounds of upstream chrome/m151 bug-fix merges land in this line, including the SubRunAllocator use-after-free fix that improves GPU text rendering stability.

bullets.lead: SKColor parsing is faster and span-friendly
bullets.detail: Parse and TryParse now use an allocation-free hex parser and add ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads, so callers can parse colors from larger buffers without slicing strings.

bullets.lead: GRContextOptions no longer requires unsafe context
bullets.detail: The type no longer carries an unsafe modifier, allowing projects to configure GPU contexts without enabling unsafe code just to reference the options class.
The per-call char[4] allocation in SKFourByteTag.Parse is removed, reducing pressure in hot parsing paths.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Added the cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness
